# Implementation Completeness Checklist: Evolution Operators (Mutation/Crossover) + Speciation + Selection

**Purpose**: Validate that all spec requirements have been correctly and completely implemented
**Created**: 2026-02-14
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Mutation Operators (US1)

- [ ] CHK001 - Are weight perturbation requirements fully addressed: both uniform (`weight += uniform(-power, +power)`) and Gaussian (`weight += N(0, power)`) distributions, configurable power (default 0.5), and weight clamping to `[WeightMinValue, WeightMaxValue]`? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [ ] CHK002 - Are weight replacement requirements fully addressed: one or more connection weights replaced with new random values drawn uniformly from `[WeightMinValue, WeightMaxValue]`? [Completeness, Spec §FR-002]
- [ ] CHK003 - Are add-connection mutation requirements fully addressed: random weight assignment, innovation ID from `IInnovationTracker.GetConnectionInnovation()`, cycle detection via DFS for feed-forward genomes, target node not input/bias, max attempts (`MaxAddConnectionAttempts`) before skip? [Completeness, Spec §FR-003]
- [ ] CHK004 - Are add-node mutation requirements fully addressed: original connection disabled, new hidden node with identity activation, incoming connection weight 1.0, outgoing connection with original weight, innovation IDs from `IInnovationTracker.GetNodeSplitInnovation()`? [Completeness, Spec §FR-004]
- [ ] CHK005 - Are toggle-enable mutation requirements fully addressed: randomly selected connection has its `IsEnabled` state flipped? [Completeness, Spec §FR-005]
- [ ] CHK006 - Is genome immutability enforced: all five mutation operators produce new `Genome` instances without modifying the original? [Completeness, Spec §FR-006]
- [ ] CHK007 - Is mutation determinism implemented: every mutation operator produces identical results given the same `Random` seed and genome state? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007]
- [ ] CHK008 - Are complexity-limit skip rules enforced: add-node skips when genome node count >= `ComplexityLimits.MaxNodes`, add-connection skips when connection count >= `ComplexityLimits.MaxConnections`, both returning the original genome unchanged? [Completeness, Spec §FR-008]
- [ ] CHK009 - Is `CompositeMutationOperator` correctly orchestrating mutations in fixed order (weight perturbation/replacement mutually exclusive first, then structural independently, then toggle-enable) with rate-based probabilistic triggering per contracts/mutation.md? [Completeness, Contract §CompositeMutationOperator]

## Crossover (US2)

- [ ] CHK010 - Are innovation-aligned crossover requirements fully addressed: two-pointer merge on connections sorted by innovation number, classifying matching/disjoint/excess genes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-009]
- [ ] CHK011 - Is matching gene inheritance implemented: randomly inherited from either parent with equal (50/50) probability? [Completeness, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK012 - Is disjoint/excess gene inheritance implemented: all such genes from the fitter parent only, with genes from both parents included when fitness is equal? [Completeness, Spec §FR-011]
- [ ] CHK013 - Is the disabled gene inheritance probability implemented: configurable probability (default 75%) that a matching gene is disabled in offspring when disabled in either parent? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK014 - Is crossover offspring validity ensured: all connection source/target IDs reference nodes in the offspring, offspring includes all input/output/bias nodes from fitter parent, offspring is immutable? [Completeness, Spec §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK015 - Is crossover determinism implemented: identical results given the same seed, parent genomes, and fitness values? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK016 - Is node inheritance correct: nodes collected from inherited connections plus all input/output/bias from fitter parent, node definitions preferred from fitter parent when referenced by both? [Completeness, Contract §NeatCrossover Node Inheritance]

## Speciation (US3)

- [ ] CHK017 - Is the compatibility distance formula correctly implemented: `d = (c1 * E / N) + (c2 * D / N) + (c3 * W)` with E=excess, D=disjoint, W=avg weight diff of matching genes, N=max(larger connection count, 1), and configurable c1/c2/c3 coefficients (defaults 1.0, 1.0, 0.4)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK018 - Is species assignment implemented: each genome compared to existing representatives, joins first species with distance < `CompatibilityThreshold` (default 3.0), otherwise creates a new species? [Completeness, Spec §FR-016]
- [ ] CHK019 - Is species representative update implemented: representative set to best-performing member (highest fitness) each generation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-017]
- [ ] CHK020 - Are stable species identifiers maintained: species retains its ID as long as it has at least one member, new species IDs are monotonically increasing? [Completeness, Spec §FR-018]
- [ ] CHK021 - Is speciation determinism implemented: identical assignments given same genome ordering, configuration, and representatives? [Completeness, Spec §FR-019]
- [ ] CHK022 - Is species lifecycle correct: members cleared but metadata preserved at generation start, empty species removed after assignment, stagnation counters updated (reset on improvement, incremented otherwise)? [Completeness, Contract §CompatibilitySpeciation]

## Selection & Reproduction (US4)

- [ ] CHK023 - Is proportional offspring allocation implemented: offspring counts proportional to species average fitness relative to total population average fitness? [Completeness, Spec §FR-020]
- [ ] CHK024 - Is elitism implemented: champion (highest-fitness member) of each species with membership >= `ElitismThreshold` (default 5) copied unchanged into next generation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-021]
- [ ] CHK025 - Is stagnation penalty implemented: species with no fitness improvement for > `StagnationThreshold` (default 15) generations receive zero offspring, while top 2 species by `BestFitnessEver` are exempt from total elimination? [Completeness, Spec §FR-022]
- [ ] CHK026 - Are all three `IParentSelector` implementations provided: tournament selection (default, configurable size, default 2), fitness-proportional roulette wheel, and stochastic universal sampling (SUS)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK027 - Is roulette wheel selection correctly handling non-positive fitness: shifting all fitnesses by `|min| + epsilon` when any fitness <= 0? [Completeness, Contract §RouletteWheelSelector]
- [ ] CHK028 - Is SUS correctly implementing evenly spaced pointers from a single random start in `[0, spacing)` with `spacing = totalFitness / numberOfSelections`? [Completeness, Contract §StochasticUniversalSamplingSelector]
- [ ] CHK029 - Is survival threshold implemented: only the top `SurvivalThreshold` fraction (default 20%) of each species' members are eligible for reproduction? [Completeness, Spec §FR-024]
- [ ] CHK030 - Is selection determinism implemented: identical parent selection and offspring allocation given the same seed, population, and configuration? [Completeness, Spec §FR-025]
- [ ] CHK031 - Is crossover rate implemented: configurable rate (default 75%) determines fraction via crossover vs clone (25%), with all offspring subsequently mutated? [Completeness, Spec §FR-025a]
- [ ] CHK032 - Is interspecies crossover implemented: configurable probability (default 0.1%) that second parent is selected from a different species? [Completeness, Spec §FR-025b]
- [ ] CHK033 - Is offspring count invariant enforced: total offspring exactly equals `PopulationSize` after rounding adjustment (largest-remainder method)? [Completeness, Contract §ReproductionAllocator]
- [ ] CHK034 - Is `ReproductionOrchestrator` correctly integrating: elitism (champions NOT mutated), survival threshold filtering, crossover vs clone decision, interspecies crossover, and mutation of all non-elite offspring? [Completeness, Contract §ReproductionOrchestrator]

## Complexity Controls (US5)

- [ ] CHK035 - Is complexity penalty implemented: `adjustedFitness = rawFitness - coefficient * complexityMeasure` with configurable coefficient and metric (NodeCount, ConnectionCount, or Both)? [Completeness, Spec §FR-026]
- [ ] CHK036 - Is complexity penalty disabled by default: coefficient = 0.0 means no fitness reduction? [Completeness, Spec §FR-026]
- [ ] CHK037 - Are hard caps from `ComplexityLimits` (Spec 01) enforced consistently with FR-008 in both add-node and add-connection mutations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-027]

## Cross-Cutting Requirements

- [ ] CHK038 - Is cross-run deterministic reproducibility implemented: all operators (mutation, crossover, speciation, selection) produce identical results across runs with the same seed and starting state? [Completeness, Spec §FR-028]
- [ ] CHK039 - Are innovation identifiers monotonically increasing within a generation and globally across the run? [Completeness, Spec §FR-029]

## Success Criteria Verification

- [ ] CHK040 - Is SC-001 testable: each mutation operator applied to a known genome with a fixed seed produces the expected structural result in 100% of test cases? [Measurability, Spec §SC-001]
- [ ] CHK041 - Is SC-002 testable: innovation IDs are monotonically increasing across 1,000 mutations with zero violations? [Measurability, Spec §SC-002]
- [ ] CHK042 - Is SC-003 testable: add-node phenotype output matches original within epsilon 1e-6 for 100 random input vectors (using `INetworkBuilder` from Spec 02)? [Measurability, Spec §SC-003]
- [ ] CHK043 - Is SC-004 testable: crossover of known parents with fixed seed produces offspring with expected gene composition for matching, disjoint, and excess genes? [Measurability, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK044 - Is SC-005 testable: identical genomes assigned to same species, structurally different genomes (distance > threshold) to different species? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK045 - Is SC-006 testable: two complete runs with same seed produce byte-identical populations at every generation? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK046 - Is SC-007 testable: species champions are structurally identical between generation N and N+1 for qualifying species over 50 generations? [Measurability, Spec §SC-007]
- [ ] CHK047 - Is SC-008 testable: stagnant species receive zero/reduced offspring, top 2 by peak fitness never eliminated? [Measurability, Spec §SC-008]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [ ] CHK048 - Is the fully-connected genome edge case handled: add-connection mutation skips and returns genome unchanged without throwing? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK049 - Is the no-enabled-connections edge case handled: add-node mutation skips and returns genome unchanged? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK050 - Is self-crossover handled: offspring is a clone with all matching genes from the single parent? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK051 - Is the all-species-stagnant edge case handled: at least top 2 species by peak fitness preserved from stagnation penalties? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK052 - Is the single-member species edge case handled: member can reproduce via mutation only, serves as representative, elitism conditional on threshold? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK053 - Are extreme compatibility threshold values handled: threshold=0.0 (every genome its own species) and very high threshold (single species) both valid without errors? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK054 - Is cycle-creating connection rejection handled: DFS from target checks if source is reachable, rejected pairs trigger retry up to `MaxAddConnectionAttempts`? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK055 - Is the unreachable-output-nodes-from-disabled-connections edge case handled: offspring is still valid, consistent with Spec 02 phenotype evaluation? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK056 - Is the zero-connections genome edge case handled: weight perturbation, weight replacement, and toggle-enable all return genome unchanged? [Coverage, Contract §WeightReplacementMutation, §ToggleEnableMutation]

## Configuration & Validation

- [ ] CHK057 - Are all NEAT-paper default values correctly set: WeightPerturbationRate=0.8, WeightReplacementRate=0.1, AddConnectionRate=0.05, AddNodeRate=0.03, ToggleEnableRate=0.01, PerturbationPower=0.5, CrossoverRate=0.75, InterspeciesCrossoverRate=0.001, DisabledGeneInheritanceProbability=0.75, c1=1.0, c2=1.0, c3=0.4, CompatibilityThreshold=3.0, ElitismThreshold=5, StagnationThreshold=15, SurvivalThreshold=0.2, TournamentSize=2? [Completeness, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK058 - Are all validation rules enforced: mutation rates in [0,1], PerturbationPower>0, WeightMinValue<WeightMaxValue, MaxAddConnectionAttempts>=1, coefficients>=0, CompatibilityThreshold>0, ElitismThreshold>=1, StagnationThreshold>=1, SurvivalThreshold in (0,1], TournamentSize>=1? [Completeness, Data Model §Validation Rules]
- [ ] CHK059 - Is `NeatSharpOptions` correctly extended with all five new option types: `Mutation`, `Crossover`, `Speciation`, `Selection`, `ComplexityPenalty`? [Completeness, Data Model §NeatSharpOptions]

## Cross-Spec Integration

- [ ] CHK060 - Is `ComplexityLimits` from Spec 01 correctly integrated: `MaxNodes` and `MaxConnections` respected by add-node and add-connection mutations? [Consistency, Spec §FR-008, §FR-027]
- [ ] CHK061 - Is `IInnovationTracker` from Spec 02 correctly integrated: `GetConnectionInnovation()` used in add-connection, `GetNodeSplitInnovation()` used in add-node, innovation IDs monotonically increasing? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, §FR-004, §FR-029]
- [ ] CHK062 - Is `Genome` immutability from Spec 02 preserved: no mutation, crossover, or selection operator modifies an existing genome instance? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, §FR-013]
- [ ] CHK063 - Is `INetworkBuilder` from Spec 02 usable for phenotype equivalence testing of add-node mutations (SC-003)? [Consistency, Spec §SC-003]

## DI Wiring & Service Registration

- [ ] CHK064 - Are all evolution services registered in `AddNeatSharp()`: five individual mutation operators (as both concrete types and `IMutationOperator`), `CompositeMutationOperator`, `NeatCrossover` as `ICrossoverOperator`, `CompatibilityDistance` as `ICompatibilityDistance`, `CompatibilitySpeciation` as `ISpeciationStrategy`, `TournamentSelector` as `IParentSelector` (default), `ReproductionAllocator`, `ReproductionOrchestrator`? [Completeness, Tasks §T045]
- [ ] CHK065 - Is the default `IParentSelector` registration `TournamentSelector`, and is it overridable via the DI container by registering a custom implementation? [Completeness, Spec §FR-023]
- [ ] CHK066 - Are all new services registered as singletons, consistent with existing registration patterns? [Consistency, Tasks §T045]

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Items reference spec sections (`Spec §FR-XXX`), success criteria (`Spec §SC-XXX`), contracts (`Contract §TypeName`), or data model definitions (`Data Model §Section`)
- 66 total items covering 29 functional requirements, 8 success criteria, 9 edge cases, configuration, cross-spec integration, and DI wiring
- Traceability: 100% of items include at least one reference to spec, contract, data model, or task source
